questions.text: Which of the following is a pure function?

options.A: A function that prints to console
options.B: A function that reads a global variable
options.C: A function whose output depends only on its arguments
options.D: A function that modifies its input

questions.explanation: A pure function has no side effects and depends solely on its arguments.

options.A: A function defined inside a class
options.B: A function that takes or returns other functions
options.C: A function with more than 3 parameters

questions.explanation: Higher-order functions accept or return functions. Examples: map, filter, reduce.

questions.text: Which concepts are core to functional programming?
questions.explanation: Immutability and recursion are fundamental to FP. Inheritance and encapsulation are OOP.

questions.text: What does map do on a list?

options.B: Reduces the list to a single value
options.C: Applies a function to each element and returns a new list

questions.explanation: map transforms each element, returning a new list of the same length.

options.B: Recursion where the recursive call is the last operation

questions.explanation: In tail recursion, the recursive call is the final operation, enabling loop optimization.
